findings.description: The SSE design assumes the existing `coordinator_task` trigger covers all work-queue transitions and can produce `from/to` transition events, but the current migration only emits for `completed`, `failed`, and `claimed`, not `running`, and the payload does not include old/new status or change_id. The required claimed→running scenario would not fire.

The audit stream contract references `AuditService.append`, but the live service exposes `log_operation` / `_insert_audit_entry`, not `append`. The design also calls for both an audit_log trigger and a service-level NOTIFY, which risks either implementation failure or duplicate events. Specify one real emission path, preferably the audit_log trigger fed by existing inserts.

`POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick` requires `change_id` to identify the registry entry, and tasks.md says to reject missing `change_id` with 422, but the spec and contracts document no request body. Implementers following the contract cannot correctly call `worktree.py teardown <change_id> --agent-id <agent_id>`.

Endpoint counts are stale and inconsistent: the architecture diagram says six new endpoints, contracts say eight, but the listed surface is nine endpoints (`events/auth`, `events/work`, `sync-points/status`, `worktrees/active`, three UI write endpoints, and two file-write endpoints). This should be normalized so work-package scope and OpenAPI coverage are unambiguous.

The force-release lock contract says to wrap existing `locks.release_lock()` with `force=True`, while proposal/design/tasks specify a new `LockService.force_release()` direct DELETE path that bypasses the holder-only RPC. The contract should align with the latter; otherwise implementation may mutate the normal release API/RPC semantics.

D10 says the coordinator owns saved-view/audit file writes to preserve the frontend boundary, but then allows Tauri to bypass the coordinator and write files directly. That bypass loses server-side header stamping and `audit_log` emission unless extra requirements are added. Remove the bypass for v1 or require Tauri to call the same coordinator endpoints.

CORS allowed headers omit the coordinator's current `X-API-Key` header, even though backward compatibility is called out in tasks and current `verify_api_key` only accepts `X-API-Key`. Browser callers using the existing header would fail preflight. Include `X-API-Key` in the allow-list or explicitly drop browser compatibility for that header.

The proposal's Out of Scope section says kick-agent writes are deferred, while the design/spec include `Kick a stale agent` and `POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick` as v1 UI behavior. Decide whether kick is in v1; current artifacts say both, which can split implementation and review expectations.
